sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_arrays (id Int32, arr1 Array(Int32), arr2 Array(Int32), value Int32) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
SELECT id FROM test_arrays WHERE hasAny(arr1, arr2) <> 0;
SELECT id, hasAny(arr1, arr2) as has_intersection FROM test_arrays GROUP BY id HAVING has_intersection <> 0;
SELECT id FROM (SELECT id, arr1, arr2 FROM test_arrays WHERE hasAny(arr1, arr2) = 1 AND arr1[1] <> 2) AS subquery WHERE value > 15;
SELECT id, arr1, arr2 FROM test_arrays WHERE hasAny(arr1, arr2) = 1 AND value <> 20 ORDER BY value DESC;
SELECT id, hasAny(arr1, arr2) as has_intersection FROM (SELECT id, arr1, arr2 FROM test_arrays WHERE arr1 IS NOT NULL AND arr2 IS NOT NULL) AS t GROUP BY id HAVING has_intersection = 1;
SELECT id FROM test_arrays t1 WHERE hasAny((SELECT arr1 FROM test_arrays t2 WHERE t1.id <> t2.id), t1.arr2) <> 0;
CREATE TABLE test_arrays_2 (id Int32, arr1 Array(String), arr2 Array(Int64), status String DEFAULT 'active') ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
SELECT id FROM test_arrays_2 WHERE hasAny(arr1, arr2) <> 0 AND status <> 'active';
SELECT id, hasAny(arr1, ['foo', 'baz']) as in_array FROM test_arrays_2 WHERE hasAny(arr1, arr2) = 1 GROUP BY id HAVING in_array = 1;
SELECT id FROM (SELECT id, arr1, arr2 FROM test_arrays_2 WHERE hasAny(arr1, arr2) = 1 AND arr1[1] <> '') AS inner_query WHERE id > 1;
